lines.line: Always wear the heaviest armour available — it has zero downside to me (no tempo/stamina/mobility cost is modelled on the wearer) and strips ~2/3 of my opponent's reach edge (REACH_W) while turning any cutter they carry into negative sigma against me (ADEF_CUT -0.9).
lines.permitting_rules: systems.py §armor_defeat_sigma(502-513) a*(cap-THRESHOLD[def.armor]); §reach_sigma(590)/§reopen(644) REACH_W[def.armor]; combatant.py armor never read as self (equipment_health=0, unwired); config WEIGHT_PEN is weapon-only
lines.what_breaks: Omega non-dominance: armour-tier choice collapses to max-plate; also flattens weapon choice vs armour

lines.line: Never play German/Chinese/Filipino — pick a counter/measure tradition (Italian/Spanish/Japanese/English): I get a free defence-to-offence riposte on 50% of any bind, a reward the bind/burst/flow schools never receive.
lines.permitting_rules: systems.py §impose_node(544-557) pd in ('counter','measure') -> bind=False,riposte=True vs pa=='bind' -> bind=True (neutral); traditions.py PREFERRED (41-42); IMPOSITION_GATE=True default
lines.what_breaks: Omega non-dominance across the tradition (lateral) choice tree; 4 of 7 traditions hold the strictly better imposition payoff

lines.line: Run a light hand-balanced weapon (rapier-class) with an aggressive disposition and the true_times ability: I commit to maximum depth every exchange for full attack power with literally zero overcommit exposure, and I always keep the aggressor role for its flat +0.12 bias.
lines.permitting_rules: systems.py §overcommit_exposure(740-743) floored product minus additive anti_overcommit + true_times(+0.25); §recoverability_factor(139-161) RECOVER_FLOOR 0.3 low for high-pc weapon; §assemble_net_sigma(689-693) ATTACKER_BIAS 0.12, WOUND_DEF_OB .25>WOUND_ATK_OB .15; DISP_INIT_K
lines.what_breaks: The stated core Omega tradeoff 'to commit IS to give up recovery' (config §69-77) — the commit-depth decision tree collapses to always-deep for this build

lines.line: Once abilities are exposed, stack every multiplicative ability across all traditions at once (mezzo_tempo x1.40, misura x1.15, atajo x1.18, staerke x1.20, plus additive indes/true_times) — nothing enforces a budget or my own tradition.
lines.permitting_rules: ability_primitives.py ability_factor/ability_bonus iterate c.equipped with no cap, no tradition check; config.py §153 + tradition_decomposition_v1.md cite an 'affinity budget' that ability_primitives does not implement; REARCHITECTURE_v1 Phase 4 unbuilt
lines.what_breaks: The vacuum-balance the imposition gate is claimed to rest on; currently inert (default empty) so a forward hazard + a false config claim

findings.claim: Heavy armour carries no wearer-side cost (no tempo/stamina/mobility/encumbrance term); it only ever modifies the opponent's exchange, so armour tier is a strictly dominant pick and its choice tree collapses.

impose_node gives counter/measure traditions a bind->riposte offensive reward while bind/burst/flow schools get a neutral or no imposition, tilting the tradition choice tree toward the 4 refuser traditions.

overcommit_exposure subtracts additive anti_overcommit + true_times from a floored multiplicative cost, driving net exposure to exactly 0 for a light hand-balanced weapon, removing the declared commit=give-up-recovery tradeoff.

The 'affinity budget' that config and the tradition-decomposition cite as balancing the imposition gate does not exist in code; ability_primitives enforces no budget and no tradition-gate, so future ability exposure permits unconstrained cross-tradition multiplicative stacking.
